title: Reasoning, Planning, Decomposition, and Verification
description: Prompt a difficult migration without exposing or demanding hidden reasoning. Learners cumulatively produce a plan-execute-verify task contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.
Use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain.
Decompose work around independently verifiable outcomes and dependency order.
